opp.ais_why:
• SF Description captures customer Michael Norris doing competitive AI discovery: 'conversation with Jaggaer this week to discuss their Agentic AI capabilities' and stating requirements for 'AI-driven, policy-compliant supplier onboarding, risk scoring, and negotiation'. Norris also asks how 'AI being utilized for autonomous negotiation agent' could change his lean-team workload ‚Äî a verbatim buyer-side AI-curiosity quote with explicit competitive AI-vendor benchmarking.
• Engaged BD, Qualified stage with 4 Avoma meetings between Nov 2025 and Mar 2026 (Jan-15 ~23min recorded, Nov-25 ~35min recorded, Mar-25 multi-attendee with Mike Aldersley) culminating in a planned KPI workshop. The Description records a 14 Jan 2026 innovation workshop that pivoted to a KPI workshop ‚Äî the deal is anchored in operational restructure under Norris's lean-team thesis.
• Product scope (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iRequest, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier, iCompliance) excludes Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA. The compelling event is a KPI-led restructure rather than an AI-driven mandate, and the Description indicates corporate posture remains exploratory with Norris personally curious about AI agents but no internal AI champion or POC on this opportunity. Score 6 (boundary DOWN per rubric) reflects competitive AI curiosity without organisational AI mandate.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
metrics.narrative: The business problem is clearly articulated but not yet quantified in pounds. Michael Norris (Global Head of Procurement) said on the 25 Nov 2025 discovery call that Jaggaer 'doesn't give us everything we need' with a 'clunkiness to their UX', and on 3 Jun 2026 he framed the goal as 'I'm just trying to supercharge everything we do' across a decentralised team of 29 procurement staff in 90-plus markets. He has drafted '20 KPIs, 16 already measurable' (15 Jan call) and wants AI to cut offshore-team error and free a lean team from admin like FSC certificate checks. No ROI, savings target, or cost-of-staying figure has been stated, so the value case is qualitative. Quantifying the cost of the Jaggaer add-on pricing model and offshore error rework is the move and feeds the July workshop.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
champion.narrative: Michael Norris is a strong, engaged single-threaded champion but not yet multi-threaded. He has driven every call, openly shares his strategy ('I'm a process guy ... technology for me is an enabler', 3 Jun), is actively running the market review, and re-engaged Zycus warmly after the RSD changeover. What is missing: he is the only confirmed buyer-side contact role on the opportunity, the economic buyer (MD of Procurement) has not been engaged, and the new Digital Enablement Lead (started week of 3 Jun, based in Cornwall) is untested. Strength rests on one person amid a parent-company demerger and a head-of-technology departure in September 2026, so the move is to broaden the relationship into the workshop attendee group before the RFI.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
competition.narrative: This is a competitive displacement of an entrenched incumbent inside an open bake-off. Jaggaer is the incumbent (in production since 2022, three modules, integrated to EcoVadis and D&B) and is aware of the review: Michael Norris met them about 'their capabilities with the relevant stakeholder' (15 Jan) and has only basic, non-agentic AI today that costs extra ('it's like another $25', 3 Jun). The open-market field named on 25 Nov was 'Zycus, Coupa, GEP and others'; Coupa is the parent DS Smith's tool but used 'in a particularly inefficient way ... signed up for 6 modules, but they only use two', so it is a low current threat in TMS's scope. The wedge is Zycus AI maturity ('714 agents'), native iCertinal eSignature 'two thirds cheaper', and the iPaaS middleware layer TMS lacks. Win by making incumbency the risky choice and shaping the RFI must-haves around these differentiators.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is concrete, repeated across three calls, and owned by Michael Norris. Confirmed pains: Jaggaer UX is 'clunky and unintuitive, requiring multiple pages' (25 Nov); contract management 'relies on email and Excel, lacking automation for renewal notifications and milestone tracking' (25 Nov); fragmented landscape of two SAP instances, two MTVT instances and legacy Asia tools with no middleware (25 Nov / 3 Jun); offshore-team human error he wants AI to suppress ('mistakes happen ... it's frustrating me', 3 Jun); decentralised sourcing managers who are 'slopey shouldered about ownership' (3 Jun); and an inability to sign supplier agreements directly creating a 'cumbersome request for signature process' (3 Jun). Inferred pain: regulatory/ESG exposure ('regulatory breach and therefore potentially a fine', 15 Jan). Pain owner is Michael Norris, the strategic-enablement lead.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly visible. Michael Norris confirmed on 3 Jun that only a statutory director can sign supplier agreements, so any deal routes through a 'request for signature process' to the MD of Procurement, and the Jaggaer incumbent contract expires April 2027 with a November 2026 renewal decision deadline acting as the forcing function. Scope is Source-to-Contract with downstream ERP integration into two SAP instances and two MTVT systems, and Michael said he will 'make this clear through the RFI'. No legal review, MSA/SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec process has been discussed yet. The RFI documentation Michael will provide (team structure, contract volumes) is the next visibility step.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits above the named contact and is not yet confirmed. Michael Norris owns the SRM/Jaggaer decision and the technology roadmap, but on the 3 Jun call he described the Managing Director of Procurement as the signing authority: 'I can't sign supplier agreements, which is mad ... it can only be a statutory director', and noted the MD 'is a ex procurement guy ... I can talk to him in a slightly different way'. That signals the MD is the likely economic buyer / sponsor but has not been engaged directly by Zycus, and no CFO/C-level has appeared on any call. Validating the approval chain and budget ceiling for the ~GBP 220k investment in the July workshop is the move; an executive connect (the deal owner's manager to the MD) should be held until Vendor Selected or Negotiation.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured open-market review and has spelled out the sequence. On the 25 Nov discovery call Michael Norris described 'an open-market evaluation ... exploring Zycus, Coupa, GEP and others'. On 3 Jun he set the path: a 2.5-hour in-person module deep-dive workshop in July with category leads and SMEs, then 'issue RFI by end of August 2026', against a hard forcing function of a Jaggaer renewal decision by November 2026 (contract to April 2027, 6-month lead time). The buying group is widening: a new Digital Enablement Lead started the week of the 3 Jun call, plus a head of technology (leaving September 2026), an ops director, supply chain lead and commercial lead are named as workshop invitees. The process is clear and buyer-owned.

sources: Avoma TMS-Zycus Update call 3 Jun 2026 (Michael Norris)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from the calls and will be formalised in the RFI. Michael Norris wants a unified, AI-native Source-to-Contract platform (not full S2P) with: ERP push integration into two SAP instances, contract auto-generation and routing toward a '90%' digital-signature target, an iPaaS/middleware layer he currently lacks, and AI that reduces offshore-team error. On 15 Jan he insisted the workshop work backwards from KPIs: 'what are your KPIs? Because if we know what the KPIs are, we will lend the AI functionality to that.' He flagged native eSignature as a real differentiator on 3 Jun after Zycus positioned iCertinal as 'two thirds cheaper'. These are confirmed buyer-stated criteria; the formal weighted criteria arrive with the RFI at end-August. Shaping the RFI 'must-have' primer is the lever.

items.detail: Close date (19 Nov 2026) is not credible given the enterprise buying motion underway. Forward path requires July workshop (~2-3 weeks), RFI end-August (~9 weeks), RFI response/eval (4-6 weeks), RFP round (4-6 weeks), vendor selection, commercial negotiation, contracting, legal/InfoSec, and SAP/MTVT integration scoping — a sequence that cannot compress into 5 months. Michael Norris confirmed Jaggaer decision deadline is November 2026 (contract expires April 2027, 6-month renewal lead time), but this is the DECISION date, not the close/implementation date. A slip to Q1 2027 is likely. The deal is healthy and progressing, but the forecast date is optimistic and creates internal misalignment risk if the close is expected in November when the realistic go-live is March 2027.
items.status: Acknowledged in verdict; must reset internal expectations and forecast to Q1 2027

items.detail: Parent-company demerger creates organizational uncertainty and potential stakeholder churn. Michael Norris confirmed TMS is rebranding to a new entity within ~12 months as part of the separation, and stated the head of technology (who aligned with Michael on the 24-month SRM roadmap) is leaving in September 2026. The new Digital Enablement Lead (started week of 3 Jun) is untested and based remotely (Cornwall), and Michael noted the relationship with the new parent-company CPO is 'a bit more closed book' than the prior relationship. While Michael stated the SRM evaluation is independent of the demerger ('we do this anyway'), organizational change increases the risk of shifting priorities, budget freezes, or stakeholder turnover that could delay or derail the project. Additionally, Michael is managing a personal house move (summer 2026) and referenced 'personal commitments over summer' as a scheduling constraint for the July workshop.

No CFO or C-level engagement yet, and budget range/approval chain is unconfirmed. Michael Norris confirmed he owns the Jaggaer renewal decision, but whether he has final commercial sign-off or must escalate to the Managing Director of Procurement, CFO, or C-level for a £220k+ procurement platform investment is unknown. The MD is supportive (per Michael) but passive and has not been engaged directly by the relevant stakeholder or C-level approval is required and surfaces late in the process (post-RFP, during commercial negotiation), it could delay close or introduce new objections (budget, ROI justification, risk appetite for switching from incumbent). Must validate approval chain and budget ceiling in the July workshop, and consider an executive connect (deal owner's manager to MD or CFO) if the deal reaches Vendor Selected or Negotiation stage.
items.status: Not addressed; gap in MEDDPICC Economic Buyer validation

items.detail: TMS procurement operates in a decentralized model with 29 staff across Europe, Americas, and Asia, and Michael Norris has noted ownership and compliance gaps ('local sourcing managers can be slopey shouldered,' 3 Jun call). Switching from Jaggaer (in production since 2022, integrated with EcoVadis/D&B, used across 3 modules) to a new platform will require change management, training, and workflow re-design across a geographically distributed team with inconsistent engagement. Michael has not discussed change management planning, user adoption strategy, or rollout phasing. If end-users resist the change or if the implementation disrupts operations, adoption could fail and the project could stall post-contract. The Digital Enablement Lead (strategic enablement role) may be tasked with driving adoption, but she is new and has not been engaged yet. Must surface change management as a topic in July and offer a structured adoption/training plan as part of the Zycus implementation approach.
items.status: Not addressed; gap in implementation planning

items.detail: Complex integration landscape with no current middleware layer. Michael Norris confirmed TMS runs two SAP instances (one ECC, one S/4HANA migration underway), two MTVT ERP instances, and has no middleware platform currently in place (3 Jun call). Zycus iPaaS has been positioned as the integration layer, but no scoping discussion has occurred: data mapping requirements, SAP/MTVT API availability, real-time vs batch sync, integration testing timeline, or go-live sequencing. Michael stated the scope is Source-to-Contract with ERP integration downstream ('push contract data into ERP'), implying contract and supplier master data must sync bidirectionally. If integration proves more complex or time-consuming than expected, it could delay go-live past the April 2027 Jaggaer expiration deadline, forcing TMS to extend Jaggaer or operate in dual-system limbo. Must validate integration feasibility and effort in July workshop with Zycus solution/technical team, and include integration scoping and testing timeline in the RFI response.
items.status: Not addressed; gap in technical scoping

items.detail: Incumbent Jaggaer has deep integration and operational continuity advantage, and will defend aggressively. Jaggaer has been in production at TMS since 2022 (3+ years), is integrated with EcoVadis and D&B, runs 3 modules (SRM, Sourcing Plus, auctions) across 29 procurement staff in 3 regions, and has institutional knowledge of TMS workflows and data structures. Michael Norris confirmed on 15 Jan that he was meeting with Jaggaer 'this week' to discuss their agentic AI capabilities, signaling Jaggaer is aware of the evaluation and will present a retention offer (likely discounted pricing, AI roadmap commitments, or UX upgrade plan). The switching cost (migration effort, training, integration rebuild, operational disruption) favors the incumbent, and if Jaggaer matches Zycus on key differentiators (AI maturity, proactive partnership, cost) or offers a steep discount, Michael may choose the lower-risk path of staying with the relevant stakeholder must make incumbent continuity the RISKY choice (by highlighting Jaggaer's structural limitations — add-on pricing model, clunky UX, reactive partnership — and quantifying the cost of staying vs switching) and make Zycus the SAFE, high-ROI choice. The July workshop and RFI criteria shaping are the only levers to overcome incumbency inertia before Jaggaer locks in retention.

hard.ais_why:
• SF Description captures customer Michael Norris doing competitive AI discovery: 'conversation with Jaggaer this week to discuss their Agentic AI capabilities' and stating requirements for 'AI-driven, policy-compliant supplier onboarding, risk scoring, and negotiation'. Norris also asks how 'AI being utilized for autonomous negotiation agent' could change his lean-team workload ‚Äî a verbatim buyer-side AI-curiosity quote with explicit competitive AI-vendor benchmarking.
• Engaged BD, Qualified stage with 4 Avoma meetings between Nov 2025 and Mar 2026 (Jan-15 ~23min recorded, Nov-25 ~35min recorded, Mar-25 multi-attendee with Mike Aldersley) culminating in a planned KPI workshop. The Description records a 14 Jan 2026 innovation workshop that pivoted to a KPI workshop ‚Äî the deal is anchored in operational restructure under Norris's lean-team thesis.
• Product scope (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iRequest, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier, iCompliance) excludes Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA. The compelling event is a KPI-led restructure rather than an AI-driven mandate, and the Description indicates corporate posture remains exploratory with Norris personally curious about AI agents but no internal AI champion or POC on this opportunity. Score 6 (boundary DOWN per rubric) reflects competitive AI curiosity without organisational AI mandate.

deltas.subject: Close date (19 Nov 2026) is not credible given the enterprise buying motion underway. Forward path requires July workshop (~2-3 weeks), RFI end-August (~9 weeks), RFI response/eval (4-6 weeks), RFP round (4-6 weeks), vendor selection, commercial negotiation, contracting, legal/InfoSec, and SAP/MTVT integration scoping — a sequence that cannot compress into 5 months. Michael Norris confirmed Jaggaer decision deadline is November 2026 (contract expires April 2027, 6-month renewal lead time), but this is the DECISION date, not the close/implementation date. A slip to Q1 2027 is likely. The deal is healthy and progressing, but the forecast date is optimistic and creates internal misalignment risk if the close is expected in November when the realistic go-live is March 2027.

deltas.to: Parent-company demerger creates organizational uncertainty and potential stakeholder churn. Michael Norris confirmed TMS is rebranding to a new entity within ~1
deltas.subject: Parent-company demerger creates organizational uncertainty and potential stakeholder churn. Michael Norris confirmed TMS is rebranding to a new entity within ~12 months as part of the separation, and stated the head of technology (who aligned with Michael on the 24-month SRM roadmap) is leaving in September 2026. The new Digital Enablement Lead (started week of 3 Jun) is untested and based remotely (Cornwall), and Michael noted the relationship with the new parent-company CPO is 'a bit more closed book' than the prior relationship. While Michael stated the SRM evaluation is independent of the demerger ('we do this anyway'), organizational change increases the risk of shifting priorities, budget freezes, or stakeholder turnover that could delay or derail the project. Additionally, Michael is managing a personal house move (summer 2026) and referenced 'personal commitments over summer' as a scheduling constraint for the July workshop.

deltas.subject: No CFO or C-level engagement yet, and budget range/approval chain is unconfirmed. deltas.to: TMS procurement operates in a decentralized model with 29 staff across Europe, Americas, and Asia, and Michael Norris has noted ownership and compliance gaps ('
deltas.subject: TMS procurement operates in a decentralized model with 29 staff across Europe, Americas, and Asia, and Michael Norris has noted ownership and compliance gaps ('local sourcing managers can be slopey shouldered,' 3 Jun call). Switching from Jaggaer (in production since 2022, integrated with EcoVadis/D&B, used across 3 modules) to a new platform will require change management, training, and workflow re-design across a geographically distributed team with inconsistent engagement. Michael has not discussed change management planning, user adoption strategy, or rollout phasing. If end-users resist the change or if the implementation disrupts operations, adoption could fail and the project could stall post-contract. The Digital Enablement Lead (strategic enablement role) may be tasked with driving adoption, but she is new and has not been engaged yet. Must surface change management as a topic in July and offer a structured adoption/training plan as part of the Zycus implementation approach.

deltas.to: Complex integration landscape with no current middleware layer. Michael Norris confirmed TMS runs two SAP instances (one ECC, one S/4HANA migration underway), t
deltas.subject: Complex integration landscape with no current middleware layer. Michael Norris confirmed TMS runs two SAP instances (one ECC, one S/4HANA migration underway), two MTVT ERP instances, and has no middleware platform currently in place (3 Jun call). Zycus iPaaS has been positioned as the integration layer, but no scoping discussion has occurred: data mapping requirements, SAP/MTVT API availability, real-time vs batch sync, integration testing timeline, or go-live sequencing. Michael stated the scope is Source-to-Contract with ERP integration downstream ('push contract data into ERP'), implying contract and supplier master data must sync bidirectionally. If integration proves more complex or time-consuming than expected, it could delay go-live past the April 2027 Jaggaer expiration deadline, forcing TMS to extend Jaggaer or operate in dual-system limbo. Must validate integration feasibility and effort in July workshop with Zycus solution/technical team, and include integration scoping and testing timeline in the RFI response.

deltas.to: Incumbent Jaggaer has deep integration and operational continuity advantage, and will defend aggressively. Jaggaer has been in production at TMS since 2022 (3+
deltas.subject: Incumbent Jaggaer has deep integration and operational continuity advantage, and will defend aggressively. Jaggaer has been in production at TMS since 2022 (3+ years), is integrated with EcoVadis and D&B, runs 3 modules (SRM, Sourcing Plus, auctions) across 29 procurement staff in 3 regions, and has institutional knowledge of TMS workflows and data structures. Michael Norris confirmed on 15 Jan that he was meeting with Jaggaer 'this week' to discuss their agentic AI capabilities, signaling Jaggaer is aware of the evaluation and will present a retention offer (likely discounted pricing, AI roadmap commitments, or UX upgrade plan). The switching cost (migration effort, training, integration rebuild, operational disruption) favors the incumbent, and if Jaggaer matches Zycus on key differentiators (AI maturity, proactive partnership, cost) or offers a steep discount, Michael may choose the lower-risk path of staying with the relevant stakeholder must make incumbent continuity the RISKY choice (by highlighting Jaggaer's structural limitations — add-on pricing model, clunky UX, reactive partnership — and quantifying the cost of staying vs switching) and make Zycus the SAFE, high-ROI choice. The July workshop and RFI criteria shaping are the only levers to overcome incumbency inertia before Jaggaer locks in retention.

deltas.to: July workshop is the single highest-leverage event to win this deal. Michael Norris has explicitly requested a 2.5-hour in-person module deep-dive before drafti
deltas.subject: July workshop is the single highest-leverage event to win this deal. Michael Norris has explicitly requested a 2.5-hour in-person module deep-dive before drafting the RFI (target end-August). The workshop must: (1) co-build the quantified value case tied to his 18 KPIs (offshore error reduction, contract automation cycle time, compliance improvement, risk visibility), (2) validate and shape the RFI criteria to favor Zycus differentiation (AI depth with 714 agents, native iPaaS middleware, iCertinal eSignature, proactive partnership via the relevant stakeholder, unified UX), and (3) recruit the new Digital Enablement Lead (started week of 3 Jun, based in Cornwall), category leads, head of tech, commercial lead, and supply chain lead as multi-threaded advocates. If this workshop does not occur or underdelivers, Jaggaer or another vendor will shape the RFI criteria and Zycus loses control of the evaluation frame. Date coordination is overdue (Michael to confirm availability by ~1 July per SF Next_Step; Zycus to share tentative dates by ~17 Jun per 3 Jun commitment).

deltas.to: Multi-thread urgently beyond Michael Norris to de-risk single-point-of-contact dependency. All 5 touchpoints (Nov 2025 to Jun 2026) have featured only Michael N
deltas.subject: Multi-thread urgently beyond Michael Norris to de-risk single-point-of-contact dependency. All 5 touchpoints (Nov 2025 to Jun 2026) have featured only Michael Norris; no other TMS stakeholder has engaged with Zycus directly. Michael has named the cross-functional buying committee (Digital Enablement Lead, category leads, head of tech, commercial lead, supply chain lead) and invited them to the July workshop, but they are currently unknown relationships. The new Digital Enablement Lead (hired for strategic enablement, not operational procurement) is a critical stakeholder who joined the week of the 3 June call — Zycus has not yet connected with her. Additionally, the current head of technology (who laid the 24-month roadmap foundations and aligned with Michael on the SRM strategy per 15 Jan call) is leaving in September 2026, creating a stakeholder transition risk. The July workshop is the opportunity to build relationships with the full buying committee, identify coaches/champions beyond Michael, and validate that the evaluation has broad internal support. A deal anchored to a single champion in a cross-functional enterprise buying motion is a rollover/delay risk, especially if Michael's priorities shift or if another vendor recruits an internal advocate.

deltas.to: Co-build and embed the quantified value case in the RFI response to differentiate from incumbent Jaggaer on ROI, not just features. Michael Norris is KPI-driven
deltas.subject: Co-build and embed the quantified value case in the RFI response to differentiate from incumbent Jaggaer on ROI, not just features. Michael Norris is KPI-driven ('without a target is a dream; I'm trying to supercharge everything we do') and has drafted 18 procurement KPIs (16 already measurable, per 15 Jan call), but no quantified cost of current inefficiency, error rate reduction target, or savings/ROI projection has been stated. He articulated clear pain (offshore team errors generating internal criticism, manual contract cycle consuming MD time, fragmented data across two SAP/two MTVT systems, decentralized ownership gaps, lack of real-time risk visibility), but the business case is still qualitative. In the July workshop, Zycus must quantify: (1) current-state waste (offshore rework hours/cost, manual contract cycle time, compliance gap frequency, MD signature bottleneck delay), (2) AI-driven savings (error reduction %, contract automation time savings, risk alert early-warning value, compliance improvement), and (3) ROI projection vs Jaggaer total cost of ownership (including Jaggaer's add-on pricing for EcoVadis/D&B, AI agentic upgrade at $25k, and reactive support model). Embed this in the RFI response as a custom ROI model tied to Michael's 18 KPIs. Without a quantified, defensible business case, the RFI evaluation defaults to feature parity and price, where Jaggaer will discount aggressively to retain and play the low-risk incumbent card. Michael's language on 3 Jun ('is Jaggaer doing what we need? No. Can it? Yeah, but at enormous cost.') signals he is open to switching but needs a compelling financial reason, not just a better UX.

value.detail: Close date (19 Nov 2026) is not credible given the enterprise buying motion underway. Forward path requires July workshop (~2-3 weeks), RFI end-August (~9 weeks), RFI response/eval (4-6 weeks), RFP round (4-6 weeks), vendor selection, commercial negotiation, contracting, legal/InfoSec, and SAP/MTVT integration scoping — a sequence that cannot compress into 5 months. Michael Norris confirmed Jaggaer decision deadline is November 2026 (contract expires April 2027, 6-month renewal lead time), but this is the DECISION date, not the close/implementation date. A slip to Q1 2027 is likely. The deal is healthy and progressing, but the forecast date is optimistic and creates internal misalignment risk if the close is expected in November when the realistic go-live is March 2027.

Michael Norris confirmed TMS runs two SAP instances (one ECC, one S/4HANA migration underway), two MTVT ERP instances, and has no middleware platform currently in place (3 Jun call). Zycus iPaaS has been positioned as the integration layer, but no scoping discussion has occurred: data mapping requirements, SAP/MTVT API availability, real-time vs batch sync, integration testing timeline, or go-live sequencing. Must validate integration feasibility and effort in July workshop with Zycus solution/technical team, and include integration scoping and testing timeline in the RFI response.

Michael Norris is KPI-driven ('without a target is a dream; I'm trying to supercharge everything we do') and has drafted 18 procurement KPIs (16 already measurable, per 15 Jan call), but no quantified cost of current inefficiency, error rate reduction target, or savings/ROI projection has been stated. He articulated clear pain (offshore team errors generating internal criticism, manual contract cycle consuming MD time, fragmented data across two SAP/two MTVT systems, decentralized ownership gaps, lack of real-time risk visibility), but the business case is still qualitative. In the July workshop, Zycus must quantify: (1) current-state waste (offshore rework hours/cost, manual contract cycle time, compliance gap frequency, MD signature bottleneck delay), (2) AI-driven savings (error reduction %, contract automation time savings, risk alert early-warning value, compliance improvement), and (3) ROI projection vs Jaggaer total cost of ownership (including Jaggaer's add-on pricing for EcoVadis/D&B, AI agentic upgrade at $25k, and reactive support model). Embed this in the RFI response as a custom ROI model tied to Michael's 18 KPIs. Without a quantified, defensible business case, the RFI evaluation defaults to feature parity and price, where Jaggaer will discount aggressively to retain and play the low-risk incumbent card. Michael's language on 3 Jun ('is Jaggaer doing what we need? No. Can it? Yeah, but at enormous cost.') signals he is open to switching but needs a compelling financial reason, not just a better UX.
